title: Fit a latent class model and write exports in one call
summary: Combines `fit` and `finalize` into a single command: estimates the LC model via EM, then immediately writes the user-facing summary and CSV exports. Emits a single started / finished event pair.
description: Reads a coded long-format choice CSV and fits a latent class logit model by the EM algorithm, then immediately finalises the run and writes the user-facing artifacts — `summary.json`, `IC.csv`, `coefficients.csv` — all in one protocol invocation. Equivalent to calling `fit` then `finalize` back-to-back, but with a single started / finished event pair instead of two. The raw EM state is persisted exactly as it would be from a standalone `fit` call, so you can still run `finalize` again separately.
usage:
[["model-mnl-lc::fit-finalize"]]
input = "<coded_long.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "lc_run"
# optional:
segments = 2
em_iterations = 200
random_seed = 0
threads = 1

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the coded long-format CSV file. Expected layout: one row per (respondent, task, alternative), with a `resp_id` column, a `task` column, a response column, and one numeric column per coded covariate.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist. Receives `spec.json`, `checkpoint.postcard`, per-iteration metrics, and an `exports/` subdirectory.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: test_input
arguments.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation.

arguments.name: export_dir
arguments.description: Where to write the CSV exports. Defaults to `{run_dir}/exports`.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSVs have a header row. Default `true`.

arguments.name: segments
arguments.description: Number of latent classes. Default `2`.

arguments.name: em_iterations
arguments.description: Maximum EM iterations. Default `200`.

arguments.name: convergence_threshold
arguments.description: EM convergence tolerance. Default `1e-5`.

arguments.name: coefficient_iterations
arguments.description: Maximum iterations for the inner-loop coefficient fit. Default `50`.

arguments.name: coefficient_precision
arguments.description: Inner-loop convergence precision. Default `1e-7`.

arguments.name: start_method
arguments.description: Initialisation strategy. Default `smart_random`.

arguments.name: use_soft_start
arguments.description: Enable simulated-annealing soft start on segment assignments.

arguments.name: softness_start
arguments.description: Initial soft-start membership softness. Only used when `use_soft_start = true`.

arguments.name: softness_end
arguments.description: Final soft-start membership softness. Only used when `use_soft_start = true`.

arguments.name: soft_start_iterations
arguments.description: Iterations over which softness decays. Only used when `use_soft_start = true`.

arguments.name: fit_error_variance
arguments.description: Fit a per-respondent scale parameter.

arguments.name: error_variance_grid
arguments.description: Comma-separated grid of scale values when `fit_error_variance = true`.

arguments.name: fixed_zero_effects
arguments.description: Force specific segment-attribute coefficients to zero.

arguments.name: random_seed
arguments.description: Random seed. `0` (default) means non-deterministic.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of threads. Default `1`.

details: `fit-finalize` is the single-call entry point for LC estimation. It runs the EM algorithm as `fit` would, then immediately calls the same finalization logic as `finalize` — all within a single protocol event pair. The raw EM state is persisted to `run_dir`, so you can still re-run `finalize` later to write exports to a different directory or re-check the summary.
value:
On success, `run_dir` contains the full EM state plus `exports/` with:

- `summary.json` — class coefficients, segment sizes, log-likelihood, information criteria.
- `IC.csv` — information criteria table.
- `coefficients.csv` — per-class coefficient table.

In protocol mode the plugin emits a single `event.run.started`, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) carrying the fit summary.
notes: Equivalent to calling `fit` then `finalize` back-to-back with the same `run_dir`.
EM is sensitive to initialisation — for model selection across segment counts, still run several seeds.

see_also.description: Fit only — call `finalize` separately when needed.
Re-summarise a completed run without re-fitting.
Cross-validated fit and finalize in one call.

examples.title: 3-class fit-finalize with soft start
examples.body:
[["model-mnl-lc::fit-finalize"]]
input = "./estimation/coded_long.csv"
run_dir = "./runs/lc-3"
run_name = "lc_run2"
segments = 3
use_soft_start = true
softness_start = 5.0
softness_end = 1.0
soft_start_iterations = 30
random_seed = 42

fields.name: input
fields.description: Path to the coded-long-format CSV file.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: test_input
fields.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation.

fields.name: export_dir
fields.description: Where to write CSV exports. Defaults to {run_dir}/exports.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: coefficient_iterations
fields.description: Inner-loop iterations for coefficient fitting. Default: 50.

fields.name: error_variance_grid
fields.description: Comma-separated grid of scale values to search over.

fields.name: fixed_zero_effects
fields.description: Force specific segment-attribute coefficients to zero.

artifacts.title: Per-class coefficient table
artifacts.description: The key output from a latent class analysis: one row per attribute level per class. Feed into choice-wrangling::expand-coefficients to generate part-worth utilities for use in a market simulator.

fields.name: coefficient
fields.description: MNL coefficient for this level within this class.

artifacts.title: Fit summary
artifacts.description: Class sizes (segment shares), log-likelihood, AIC, BIC, and the full coefficient table in JSON form.

artifacts.title: Information criteria table
artifacts.description: Per-segment information criteria for assessing how many classes to use.
